title: Dombeya triflora
taxonomy: Dombeya triflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Dombeya, which comprises over 100 species of flowering plants. The plant is native to tropical Africa, where it is commonly found in wooded areas and along rivers. The classification of Dombeya triflora is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Malvales, Family: Malvaceae, Genus: Dombeya, Species: D. triflora. The classification of Dombeya triflora is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup.
uses: Dombeya triflora has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
